---
title: "Observer Analysis"
author: "My Ha"
date: "2026-03-26"
output: html_document
---
```{r}
# Install packages 
#install.packages(c("tidyverse", "scales"))
install.packages("readxl")

# Load libraries
library(tidyverse)  # for data manipulation and plotting
library(scales)     # for percent formatting on x-axis
library(readxl)
```

```{r}
# Read CSV
df_sheet3 <- read_excel("Observer Master.xlsx", sheet = 3)

# Quick check
glimpse(df_sheet3)
```
```{r}
# Quick check
colnames(df_sheet3)
```

```{r}
#Calculate period midpoint & normalized time
df_sheet3 <- df_sheet3 %>%
  mutate(period_mid = (`Start Year` + `End Year`)/2) %>%
  group_by(President) %>%
  mutate(norm_time = (period_mid - min(`Start Year`)) / (max(`End Year`) - min(`Start Year`))) %>%
  ungroup()
```

```{r}
#fix missing values
df_sheet3 <- df_sheet3 %>%
  filter(!is.na(Progress), !is.na(period_mid))
```


```{r}
#Aggregate progress per period
df_summary <- df_sheet3 %>%
  group_by(President, period_mid, norm_time) %>%
  summarise(avg_progress = mean(Progress), .groups = "drop")
```

```{r}
#Plot normalized progress line chart
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)  # for percent formatting on x-axis

ggplot(df_summary, aes(x = norm_time, y = avg_progress, color = President, group = President)) +
  geom_line(size = 1.2) +       # line for each president
  geom_point(size = 3) +        # points at each period
  scale_x_continuous(labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +  # 0% → 100%
  labs(
    x = "Presidential Timeline (%)",
    y = "Average Progress",
    title = "Power Progress of Presidents Over Normalized Time"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )
```


```{r}
df_clean <- df_sheet3 %>%
  filter(!is.na(Progress), !is.na(period_mid)) %>%
  group_by(President, period_mid) %>%
  summarise(Progress = mean(Progress), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(President = factor(President, levels = c("Ber", "Sor", "Tre", "Bah"))) %>%  # <-- added
  arrange(period_mid)

ggplot(df_clean, aes(x = period_mid, y = Progress, color = President)) +
  geom_point(size = 4) +
  geom_line(aes(group = 1), color = "black", linewidth = 1) +
  labs(
    title = "Power Progress over Time",
    x = "Time",
    y = "Power Progress"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 4)) + 
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
```

```{r}
df_clean <- df_sheet3 %>%
  filter(!is.na(Progress), !is.na(period_mid)) %>%
  group_by(President, period_mid) %>%
  summarise(Progress = mean(Progress), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(President = factor(President, levels = c("Ber", "Sor", "Tre", "Bah"))) %>%
  arrange(period_mid)

ggplot(df_clean, aes(x = period_mid, y = Progress, color = President)) +
  geom_line(aes(group = 1), color = "#4D4D4D", linewidth = 1.05) +
  geom_point(size = 3.8) +
  labs(
    title = "Power Progress over Time",
    subtitle = "Average progress score across presidential periods",
    x = "Time",
    y = "Power Progress",
    color = NULL,
    caption = "Source: Augustana archival analysis"
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(
    "Ber" = "#1F5A94",
    "Sor" = "#D97A2B",
    "Tre" = "#4C9A2A",
    "Bah" = "#C43C39"
  )) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 4),
    breaks = seq(0, 4, 1),
    expand = c(0, 0)
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(
      face = "bold",
      size = 16,
      hjust = 0,
      margin = margin(b = 5)
    ),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(
      size = 11,
      color = "#555555",
      hjust = 0,
      margin = margin(b = 12)
    ),
    plot.caption = element_text(
      size = 9.5,
      color = "#666666",
      hjust = 0,
      margin = margin(t = 10)
    ),
    axis.title = element_text(
      face = "bold",
      size = 11.5,
      color = "#222222"
    ),
    axis.text.x = element_text(
      size = 10.5,
      color = "#444444"
    ),
    axis.text.y = element_text(
      size = 10.5,
      color = "#444444"
    ),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(
      color = "#D9D9D9",
      linewidth = 0.4
    ),
    axis.ticks = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "top",
    legend.text = element_text(
      size = 10.5,
      color = "#333333"
    ),
    plot.margin = margin(18, 24, 18, 12)
  )

ggsave("observer_figures/figure2.png", width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 300)
```


```{r}
ggplot(df_sheet3, aes(x = norm_time, y = Progress)) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = FALSE, color = "blue", size = 1.5) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
  expand_limits(y = 0) +   # ensures y-axis starts at 0
  labs(
    x = "Presidential Timeline (%)",
    y = "Average Progress",
    title = "Overall Power Progress Across All Presidents"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
```
```{r}
#Avg granted over time
df_granted <- df_sheet3 %>%
  filter(!is.na(Granted), !is.na(period_mid)) %>%
  group_by(President, period_mid) %>%
  summarise(Granted = mean(Granted), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(President = factor(President, levels = c("Ber", "Sor", "Tre", "Bah"))) %>%
  arrange(period_mid)

ggplot(df_granted, aes(x = period_mid, y = Granted, color = President)) +
  geom_line(aes(group = 1), color = "#4D4D4D", linewidth = 1.05) +
  geom_point(size = 3.8) +
  labs(
    title = "Granted Power over Time",
    subtitle = "Average level of power granted to students across presidential periods",
    x = "Time",
    y = "Average Granted Power",
    color = NULL,
    caption = "Source: Augustana archival analysis"
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(
    "Ber" = "#1F5A94",
    "Sor" = "#D97A2B",
    "Tre" = "#4C9A2A",
    "Bah" = "#C43C39"
  )) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 5),   # <-- IMPORTANT: Granted likely goes to 5
    breaks = seq(0, 5, 1),
    expand = c(0, 0)
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(expand = c(0.02, 0)) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16, hjust = 0, margin = margin(b = 5)),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 11, color = "#555555", hjust = 0, margin = margin(b = 12)),
    plot.caption = element_text(size = 9.5, color = "#666666", hjust = 0, margin = margin(t = 10)),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11.5, color = "#222222"),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 10.5, color = "#444444"),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color = "#D9D9D9", linewidth = 0.4),
    axis.ticks = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "top",
    legend.text = element_text(size = 10.5),
    plot.margin = margin(18, 18, 18, 12)
  )

ggsave("observer_figures/figure1.png", width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 300)
```

   
```{r}
#Granted and Gained by President
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)

df_bar <- df_sheet3 %>%
  group_by(President) %>%
  summarise(
    Granted = mean(Granted, na.rm = TRUE),
    Gained  = mean(Gained, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(Granted, Gained),
    names_to = "Power_Type",
    values_to = "Value"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Power_Type = factor(Power_Type, levels = c("Granted", "Gained")),
    President = factor(President, levels = c("Ber", "Sor", "Tre", "Bah"))
  )

ggplot(df_bar, aes(x = President, y = Value, fill = Power_Type)) +
  geom_col(
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.72),
    width = 0.56
  ) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = round(Value, 1)),
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.72),
    vjust = -0.35,
    size = 4,
    color = "#222222"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "Granted" = "#1F5A94",
    "Gained"  = "#D97A2B"
  )) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 5.2),
    breaks = seq(0, 5, 1),
    expand = c(0, 0)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Granted vs. Gained Power by President",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Average Power Score",
    fill = NULL,
    caption = "Source: Augustana archival analysis"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(
      face = "bold",
      size = 16,
      hjust = 0,
      margin = margin(b = 5)
    ),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(
      size = 11,
      color = "#555555",
      hjust = 0,
      margin = margin(b = 12)
    ),
    plot.caption = element_text(
      size = 9.5,
      color = "#666666",
      hjust = 0,
      margin = margin(t = 10)
    ),
    axis.title.y = element_text(
      face = "bold",
      size = 11.5,
      color = "#222222"
    ),
    axis.text.x = element_text(
      size = 11,
      color = "#222222"
    ),
    axis.text.y = element_text(
      size = 10.5,
      color = "#555555"
    ),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(
      color = "#D9D9D9",
      linewidth = 0.4
    ),
    axis.ticks = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "top",
    legend.text = element_text(
      size = 10.5,
      color = "#333333"
    ),
    plot.margin = margin(18, 18, 18, 12)
  )

ggsave("observer_figures/figure3.png", width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 300)

```


```{r}
# most common actors
actor_counts <- df_sheet3 %>%
  summarise(
    `Student government leaders or members` = sum(`Student government leaders or members` == "T", na.rm = TRUE),
    `Individual students` = sum(`Individual students` == "T", na.rm = TRUE),
    `Student organizations or groups` = sum(`Student organizations or groups` == "T", na.rm = TRUE),
    `Alumni` = sum(`Alumni` == "T", na.rm = TRUE),
    `Administrators` = sum(`Administrators` == "T", na.rm = TRUE),
    `Faculty` = sum(`Faculty` == "T", na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "Actor", values_to = "Count")

ggplot(actor_counts, aes(x = reorder(Actor, Count), y = Count)) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Most Common Actors",
    x = "Actors",
    y = "Number of Mentions"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
```

```{r}
# install once if needed
# install.packages("kableExtra")
# install.packages("webshot2")

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(kableExtra)
library(webshot2)

# -----------------------------
# Build table (Top 3 actors by %)
# -----------------------------
actor_top3_table <- df_sheet3 %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(
      `Student government leaders or members`,
      `Individual students`,
      `Student organizations or groups`,
      `Alumni`,
      `Administrators`,
      `Faculty`
    ),
    names_to = "Actor",
    values_to = "Mentioned"
  ) %>%
  filter(Mentioned == "T") %>%
  group_by(President, Actor) %>%
  summarise(Count = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  left_join(
    df_sheet3 %>%
      group_by(President) %>%
      summarise(Total_Events = n(), .groups = "drop"),
    by = "President"
  ) %>%
  mutate(Percent = Count / Total_Events * 100) %>%
  group_by(President) %>%
  arrange(desc(Percent), .by_group = TRUE) %>%
  slice_head(n = 3) %>%
  mutate(Rank = c("Top 1", "Top 2", "Top 3")) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(
    Actor_with_pct = paste0(Actor, " (", round(Percent, 1), "%)")
  ) %>%
  select(Rank, President, Actor_with_pct) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = President, values_from = Actor_with_pct) %>%
  select(Rank, Ber, Sor, Tre, Bah)

# -----------------------------
# Color mapping
# -----------------------------
actor_colors <- c(
  "Student government leaders or members" = "#4E79A7",
  "Individual students" = "#F28E2B",
  "Student organizations or groups" = "#59A14F",
  "Alumni" = "#E15759",
  "Administrators" = "#B07AA1",
  "Faculty" = "#76B7B2"
)

# Function to make a smaller "pill" style label
pill_cell <- function(text) {
  actor_name <- sub(" \\(.*\\)", "", text)
  bg <- actor_colors[actor_name]

  if (is.na(bg)) return(text)

  cell_spec(
    text,
    format = "html",
    background = bg,
    color = "white",
    bold = TRUE,
    extra_css = paste(
      "display:inline-block;",
      "padding:4px 8px;",
      "border-radius:12px;",
      "font-size:13px;",
      "line-height:1.25;"
    )
  )
}

# Apply pill styling
table_colored <- actor_top3_table %>%
  mutate(
    Ber = sapply(Ber, pill_cell),
    Sor = sapply(Sor, pill_cell),
    Tre = sapply(Tre, pill_cell),
    Bah = sapply(Bah, pill_cell)
  )

# -----------------------------
# Render table (clean / Pew-like)
# -----------------------------
kbl(
  table_colored,
  format = "html",
  escape = FALSE,
  align = "lcccc",
  caption = "Top Three Most Common Actors by Presidency"
) %>%
  kable_styling(
    full_width = FALSE,
    position = "left",
    font_size = 14
  ) %>%
  add_header_above(c(" " = 1, "Presidency" = 4), bold = FALSE) %>%
  row_spec(0, bold = TRUE, extra_css = "border-bottom: 1px solid #D9D9D9;") %>%
  row_spec(1:3, extra_css = "vertical-align: middle; height: 56px;") %>%
  column_spec(1, width = "90px", bold = TRUE) %>%
  column_spec(2:5, width = "220px") %>%
  column_spec(2:5, extra_css = "text-align:center;") %>%
  footnote(
    general = "Entries show the top three actors in each presidency, based on share of events.",
    general_title = "Note: "
  )

# -----------------------------
# Save table as PNG
# -----------------------------
dir.create("observer_figures", showWarnings = FALSE)

save_kable(
  kbl(
    table_colored,
    format = "html",
    escape = FALSE,
    align = "lcccc",
    caption = "Top Three Most Common Actors by Presidency"
  ) %>%
    kable_styling(
      full_width = FALSE,
      position = "left",
      font_size = 14,
      bootstrap_options = c("striped", "condensed")
    ) %>%
    row_spec(0, bold = TRUE) %>%
    row_spec(1:3, extra_css = "vertical-align: middle;") %>%
    column_spec(1, width = "90px", bold = TRUE) %>%
    column_spec(2:5, width = "210px") %>%
    add_header_above(c(" " = 1, "Presidency" = 4)),
  file = "observer_figures/table.html"
)

webshot(
  "observer_figures/table.html",
  "observer_figures/top_actors_table.png",
  zoom = 2,
  selector = "table"
)
```


```{r}
#convert topic columns into a long format, then attach the granted value.
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

topic_granted <- df_sheet3 %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(
      `Academic Policies`,
      `Campus Life`,
      `Student Government`,
      `Financial/Administrative Decisions`,
      `Diversity, Equity & Inclusion`,
      `Activism/Social Movements`,
      `Facilities/Infrastructure`,
      `Faculty/Staff Relations`
    ),
    names_to = "Topic",
    values_to = "Mentioned"
  ) %>%
  filter(Mentioned == "T")
```

```{r}
#Aggregate “Granted” by topic
topic_power <- topic_granted %>%
  group_by(Topic) %>%
  summarise(
    Granted_Power = sum(Granted, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
```


```{r}
# Avg Granted by Topic
topic_power_avg <- topic_granted %>%
  group_by(Topic) %>%
  summarise(
    Avg_Granted = mean(Granted, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

ggplot(topic_power_avg, aes(x = Avg_Granted, y = reorder(Topic, Avg_Granted))) +
  geom_col(
    fill = "#1F5A94",
    width = 0.55
  ) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = round(Avg_Granted, 2)),
    hjust = -0.15,
    size = 4,
    color = "#222222"
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Average Granted Power by Topic Area",
    subtitle = "Mean level of power granted across topic areas",
    x = NULL,
    y = NULL,
    caption = "Source: Augustana archival analysis"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    limits = c(0, max(topic_power_avg$Avg_Granted) + 0.5),
    breaks = seq(0, 5, 1),
    expand = c(0, 0)
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(
      face = "bold",
      size = 16,
      hjust = 0,
      margin = margin(b = 5)
    ),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(
      size = 11,
      color = "#555555",
      hjust = 0,
      margin = margin(b = 12)
    ),
    plot.caption = element_text(
      size = 9.5,
      color = "#666666",
      hjust = 0,
      margin = margin(t = 10)
    ),
    axis.text.y = element_text(
      size = 11,
      color = "#222222"
    ),
    axis.text.x = element_text(
      size = 10.5,
      color = "#555555"
    ),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_line(
      color = "#D9D9D9",
      linewidth = 0.4
    ),
    axis.ticks = element_blank(),
    plot.margin = margin(18, 24, 18, 12)
  )

ggsave("observer_figures/figure4.png", width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 300)
```


```{r}
##convert topic columns into a long format, then attach the granted value.
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

topic_progress <- df_sheet3 %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(
      `Academic Policies`,
      `Campus Life`,
      `Student Government`,
      `Financial/Administrative Decisions`,
      `Diversity, Equity & Inclusion`,
      `Activism/Social Movements`,
      `Facilities/Infrastructure`,
      `Faculty/Staff Relations`
    ),
    names_to = "Topic",
    values_to = "Mentioned"
  ) %>%
  filter(Mentioned == "T")
```


```{r}
#Aggregate “Power Progress” by topic
topic_progress_summary <- topic_progress %>%
  group_by(Topic) %>%
  summarise(
    Total_Progress = sum(Progress, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
```


```{r}
# Avg Power Progress by Topic
topic_progress_avg <- topic_progress %>%
  group_by(Topic) %>%
  summarise(
    Avg_Progress = mean(Progress, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

ggplot(topic_progress_avg, aes(x = Avg_Progress, y = reorder(Topic, Avg_Progress))) +
  geom_col(
    fill = "#1B9E77",
    width = 0.55
  ) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = round(Avg_Progress, 2)),
    hjust = -0.15,
    size = 4,
    color = "#222222"
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Average Power Progress by Topic Area",
    subtitle = "Mean level of progress achieved across topic areas",
    x = NULL,
    y = NULL,
    caption = "Source: Augustana archival analysis"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    limits = c(0, max(topic_progress_avg$Avg_Progress) + 0.5),
    breaks = seq(0, 5, 1),
    expand = c(0, 0)
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(
      face = "bold",
      size = 16,
      hjust = 0,
      margin = margin(b = 5)
    ),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(
      size = 11,
      color = "#555555",
      hjust = 0,
      margin = margin(b = 12)
    ),
    plot.caption = element_text(
      size = 9.5,
      color = "#666666",
      hjust = 0,
      margin = margin(t = 10)
    ),
    axis.text.y = element_text(
      size = 11,
      color = "#222222"
    ),
    axis.text.x = element_text(
      size = 10.5,
      color = "#555555"
    ),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_line(
      color = "#D9D9D9",
      linewidth = 0.4
    ),
    axis.ticks = element_blank(),
    plot.margin = margin(18, 24, 18, 12)
  )

ggsave("observer_figures/figure5.png", width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 300)
```
